name: Amber Sunrise Espresso
notes: Brown sugar, orange peel, toasted almond
description: A medium-roast single origin grown at 1,700m, washed and dried slowly on raised beds. Bright acidity balanced by a syrupy body — built for espresso but forgiving on a moka pot.

name: Quiet Hills Decaf
description: Sugarcane-process decaf that keeps its roundness without the caffeine. Roasted a shade darker to bring out cocoa and cedar.

name: Midfield Sencha
description: First-flush sencha, steamed and rolled by hand. Steep short and cool for a clean, vegetal cup.

name: Late Frost Oolong
description: High-mountain oolong picked after the first cold snap, which concentrates sugars in the leaf. Rewards multiple steeps.

name: Smoked Paprika, Whole Pod
description: Pimentón de la Vera, oak-smoked over three weeks the traditional way. Grind fresh for stews, rubs, and roasted vegetables.

name: Green Cardamom Pods
description: Hand-sorted for size and oil content. Crack a pod into rice, coffee, or a simmering chai.

name: Aged Sherry Vinegar
description: Solera-aged a minimum of 12 years in American oak. A few drops finishes a soup, a salad, or a pan sauce.

name: Stone-Ground Polenta
description: Coarse-ground heirloom corn, milled in small batches so the germ stays intact. Slow-cooked polenta with real corn flavor.

name: Wildflower Honey
description: Raw and unfiltered, pulled from hives that forage across an unsprayed hillside. Crystallizes naturally — that's how you know it's real.

name: Ceylon Cinnamon Quills
description: True cinnamon, hand-rolled in thin layers. Softer and more citrusy than cassia — good enough to eat off the spoon.

name: Harvest Blend Chai
description: Full-leaf Assam blended with whole spices in small batches. Simmer with milk for a chai that actually tastes like the spices you put in.

name: Nightfall Dark Roast
description: Wet-hulled and roasted dark for weight and low acidity. The one for a French press on a cold morning.
